title: Phase 6 — The Medical Team (local-first, code-enforced health/wellness team)
description: Add a second domain Team instance to agent-bober: a local-first, conversational health/wellness team that validates the domain-agnostic Team abstraction on a high-stakes domain. Introduces a new additive `medical-sop` pipelineShape with a dedicated engine running a fixed SOP (consent -> red-flag-gate -> numerics -> retrieve -> answer-with-abstention), a concrete GuardrailSet filling the existing Team.guardrails slot, an in-process whitelisted numerics layer (no Python sandbox), and two independently opt-in egress axes. Regulatory refusals, the emergency short-circuit, numerical reasoning, and the zero-egress default are CODE-ENFORCED, never prompt-only — the highest-stakes decisions never pass through an LLM. Built from arch-20260616-medical-team. Engineering-complete on a feature branch; shipping/enabling remains gated on an external S6.5 counsel/regulatory review which is NOT a buildable sprint.

resolvedClarifications.answer: Full surface in this plan, including a REAL networked grounded-literature source (not just the abstain/disabled interface). ~7 sprints.

MedlinePlus / NIH open APIs (consumer-health, no OAuth). Lower legal/integration risk than PubMed/PMC; accessed only behind the `literature-retrieval` egress axis; CI uses recorded/fixture responses, no live network.

Apple Health XML only (SAX streaming, the named ~4GB case), plus the IngestionAdapter registry so additional sources (Whoop/CSV) are additive later. Whoop and CSV adapters are out of scope for this plan.

Medical team is REGISTERED and selectable via `bober chat medical`, but both egress axes default false and first-run consent is fail-closed. Engineering-complete but ships nothing to cloud by default; shipping/marketing remains gated on the external S6.5 counsel review.

assumptions: The `bober/team-abstraction` substrate is merged to main (verified: commit 63e6a33 '#45'); the Team interface (src/teams/types.ts:20), loadTeam (src/teams/registry.ts:34), PipelineEngineName (src/orchestrator/workflow/engine.ts:6), and both exhaustive switches in src/orchestrator/workflow/selector.ts (selectPipelineEngine:51, selectPipelineEngineForTeam:103) are present on HEAD. The architecture's shorthand paths `selector.ts`/`engine.ts` resolve to src/orchestrator/workflow/.

Adding `medical-sop` to PipelineEngineName forces an exhaustiveness update in BOTH switches in selector.ts: selectPipelineEngineForTeam returns the MedicalSopEngine; selectPipelineEngine (the config/programming path, which reads config.pipeline.engine and can never legitimately be `medical-sop`) handles the new member defensively (e.g. falls through to TsPipelineEngine or throws) so the switch stays exhaustive without changing programming behavior.

The `pipelineShape` Zod enum must be widened to include `medical-sop` everywhere it mirrors PipelineEngineName: the team pipelineShape enum (src/config/schema.ts:366) and the PipelineSectionSchema engine enum the engine.ts comment references. These must stay in sync with the TS union.

Team.guardrails is currently typed `?: unknown` (src/teams/types.ts:29); filling it with a concrete GuardrailSet type is additive/narrowing and breaks nothing for existing teams that leave it undefined.

MedicalSopEngine `implements PipelineEngine` (PipelineEngine is an interface at engine.ts:9, not an abstract class — the architecture's `extends` was shorthand). Its run signature matches `run(userPrompt, projectRoot, config, opts?: { runId?: string }): Promise<PipelineResult>`.

Medical chat turns route through the EXISTING detached-spawn contract (ADR-5): TurnClassifier -> action 'spawn' -> RunSpawner launches a detached child that returns SpawnAck immediately; the SOP runs inside that child via selectPipelineEngineForTeam. No synchronous inline answer is added to ChatSession.handleTurn (it must stay non-blocking).

All numerical reasoning is deterministic in-process TypeScript over a closed NumericPrimitive whitelist (mean|min|max|latest|delta|slope|percentile|zscore). No eval, no code generation, no Python sandbox. The LLM never performs arithmetic.

HealthDataStore uses better-sqlite3 SYNC (mirroring FactStore) with a single generic events table and INSERT OR IGNORE on a deterministic SHA-256 id of (metric|tStart|source|value); all other I/O uses node:fs/promises. All new code is ESM/.js/NodeNext, strict TS, provider-agnostic (no SDK types outside src/providers/).

Timestamps are injected parameters everywhere (observation tStart, consent acceptedAtIso, audit tIso) and are NEVER read from the wall clock — matching the architecture data-model note and keeping tests deterministic.

Both egress axes (cloud-inference, literature-retrieval) default false. The medical team is registered/selectable but ships nothing to cloud by default; the local Ollama model (via openai-compat, src/providers/factory.ts) is the default synthesize model with NO automatic cloud fallback.

MedlinePlus / NIH retrieval requires no OAuth; unit/integration tests use recorded/fixture HTTP responses (no live network in CI), satisfying both local-first and the 'no fs/network mocks beyond fixtures' principle.

This plan delivers engineering-complete code on a feature branch; the external S6.5 FFDCA §201(h) counsel/regulatory review that gates shipping is NOT a buildable component and is explicitly out of scope for code.
outOfScope: The S6.5 counsel/regulatory review itself (external process, release-gating, not buildable; shipping/marketing the medical team is blocked on it regardless of engineering completeness).
Whoop and CSV ingestion adapters (Apple Health XML only; the IngestionAdapter registry makes additional sources additive later).

A built/tested cloud-inference synthesis path beyond the EgressGuard axis existing and defaulting off (provider-specific cloud synth is reachable only after the independent cloud-inference opt-in; not exercised in this plan).
Multi-user, server, or throughput concerns — single-user interactive `bober chat`/`bober medical` only.
New numeric primitives beyond the 8 whitelisted ones (added later via reviewable code change, never via runtime/generated code).
Any UI/GUI (CLI + chat only, per project principles — agent-bober has no UI).
Cloud-inference prompt-cache economics (deferred per architecture Open Question).
Any change to the ts|skill|workflow engines or the programming team's behavior (byte-zero invariant is a hard requirement).
Medication PARSING from ingested files (medications are the FactStore value-of-record per ADR-7; HealthDataStore never stores medication-list state).

features.title: Additive pipeline plumbing + medical team registration
features.description: Add `medical-sop` as a new additive PipelineEngineName + Zod enum member, extend both exhaustive switches in selector.ts, create a minimal MedicalSopEngine stub implementing PipelineEngine, define the GuardrailSet type filling Team.guardrails, and register a built-in `medical` team via buildMedicalTeam(config) + loadTeam so `bober chat medical` resolves to the medical SOP — with byte-zero impact on existing teams.
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: PipelineEngineName (src/orchestrator/workflow/engine.ts) includes `medical-sop`, and every Zod enum mirroring it (team pipelineShape at schema.ts:366 + the PipelineSectionSchema engine enum) is widened in lockstep; typecheck and build pass.

AC2: Both switches in selector.ts handle the new member exhaustively (compile-time `never` guard intact): selectPipelineEngineForTeam returns a MedicalSopEngine for a `medical-sop` team; selectPipelineEngine (config/programming path) handles it defensively without altering ts/skill/workflow behavior.

AC3: loadTeam(config, 'medical') returns a Team with pipelineShape === 'medical-sop' and a concrete GuardrailSet in its guardrails slot; loadTeam(config) (no id) still returns the byte-identical programming team.

AC4: The existing ts|skill|workflow engines and the programming team behave identically — the full existing test suite stays green and a regression test asserts the programming team still resolves to its prior engine.

features.title: Consent gate, append-only audit log, and disclaimer footer
features.description: Fail-closed first-run ConsentGate (absent consent => refuse, no downstream calls), an append-only AuditLog (.bober/medical/audit-<date>.jsonl, O_APPEND|O_CREAT, mode 0600, IDs/enums only — never prompt text or health values), and a versioned DisclaimerComposer footer. ConsentGate is wired as Gate 1 in MedicalSopEngine.run.
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: When no consent is recorded, MedicalSopEngine.run returns a refuse verdict and makes ZERO downstream calls (no numerics, no retrieval, no LLM); a recorded consent round-trips via ConsentGate.current().

AC2: AuditLog.append writes to .bober/medical/audit-<date>.jsonl with file mode 0600 and O_APPEND semantics, and a test asserts the serialized entry contains only IDs/enums (event, rulesetVersion, patternsetVersion, ruleId) and NO prompt text or health values.
AC3: DisclaimerComposer.footer() returns a non-empty string carrying disclaimerVersion; the version is recorded in the ConsentRecord.
AC4: All timestamps (consent acceptedAtIso, audit tIso) are injected parameters, not read from the system clock (verified by a deterministic test).

features.title: Red-flag emergency short-circuit (Gate 2, 0 LLM calls)
features.description: A deterministic, pure/synchronous RedFlagDetector (categories cardiac|stroke|anaphylaxis|self-harm|overdose|none, with patternsetVersion) wrapped by GuardrailSet.evaluate (allow|short-circuit|refuse, with rulesetVersion), wired into MedicalSopEngine.run immediately after the consent gate. A red-flag match returns a canned 911/988 escalation with zero downstream calls.
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: For a prompt in each red-flag category, GuardrailSet.evaluate returns a short-circuit verdict with a canned escalation and the engine makes 0 LLM calls — verified with a spy/fake LLMClient asserted never to be invoked.
AC2: RedFlagDetector.detect is pure and synchronous: identical input yields identical output across runs, and a non-emergency prompt returns category 'none' and proceeds to allow.
AC3: A short-circuit appends an audit entry carrying ruleId, rulesetVersion, and patternsetVersion (IDs/enums only).
AC4: GuardrailSet.evaluate throws on an empty prompt; the detector requires no network and no model.

features.title: HealthDataStore + deterministic NumericsQueryLayer
features.description: A generic single-table HealthDataStore (better-sqlite3 sync, INSERT OR IGNORE on deterministic SHA-256 id, getObservations/getLabSeries/getBaseline/putBaseline/getPreference) and a NumericsQueryLayer exposing only the closed NumericPrimitive whitelist (mean|min|max|latest|delta|slope|percentile|zscore) and getLabTrend. The LLM never performs arithmetic; sampleCount 0 => abstain.
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: HealthDataStore.upsertObservations dedupes identical observations via INSERT OR IGNORE on the deterministic id and returns the count of NEW rows only (test inserts duplicates and asserts the count).

AC2: Each of the 8 numeric primitives computes the correct value on a fixture series; getMetric with an empty window returns NumericResult { value: null, sampleCount: 0 } (abstain) rather than throwing.
AC3: NumericsQueryLayer reads the per-row unit and refuses to aggregate across heterogeneous units rather than silently mixing them.
AC4: No eval, Function constructor, code generation, or child process appears anywhere in src/medical numerics code (verified by grep/test).

features.title: Apple Health ingestion (SAX streaming) + `bober medical import`
features.description: An IngestionNormalizer with an IngestionAdapter registry and ObservationSink, plus an AppleHealthAdapter that stream-parses ~4GB Apple Health export XML via SAX with bounded (~1000-row) batches and pause/resume backpressure, never loading the whole document. A `bober medical import <file>` CLI command drives it.
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: AppleHealthAdapter ingests a fixture Apple Health XML via streaming SAX with bounded batches (batch-size cap asserted) and never reads the whole file into memory; writeBatch applies backpressure (pause/resume).
AC2: Re-importing the same file is idempotent — the second import adds 0 new rows (dedup via HealthDataStore INSERT OR IGNORE).
AC3: IngestionNormalizer.importFile throws a clear error naming the problem when no registered adapter canHandle the file.
AC4: `bober medical import <file>` is registered, callable, and reports an IngestionResult (counts) on completion.

features.title: EgressGuard + medications-in-FactStore + full SOP wiring (zero-egress end-to-end)
features.description: EgressGuard with two independently opt-in axes (cloud-inference, literature-retrieval, both default false; assertAllowed throws when off) plus a `no-restricted-imports` ESLint boundary scoped to src/medical/**/*.ts. Medications read from FactStore.getActiveFacts (bi-temporal, ADR-7), not HealthDataStore. The full ordered SOP is wired in MedicalSopEngine.run: consent -> red-flag -> numerics -> meds -> egress check -> (retriever disabled => abstain) -> footer -> audit -> PipelineResult.
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: EgressGuard.assertAllowed throws when an axis is off and returns/passes when on; both axes default false. With literature-retrieval OFF, LiteratureRetriever.retrieve returns { disabled } synchronously (no network attempt) and the engine abstains.

AC2: A `files: ['src/medical/**/*.ts']` no-restricted-imports ESLint block forbids undici/got/axios/node-fetch/node:http(s)/node:net/node:tls and the fetch global everywhere in src/medical EXCEPT the one designated retrieval network file; lint passes.
AC3: Medications are read via FactStore.getActiveFacts under a medical scope (value-of-record), and HealthDataStore is never used to store medication-list state.

AC4: With both egress axes off, a full medical SOP turn produces a MedicalAnswer with a disclaimer footer and an audit answer/abstain entry, and a numeric question is answered purely from deterministic compute — default outbound bytes = 0 (no network module reachable).

features.title: MedlinePlus grounded retrieval + cited synthesis (opt-in)
features.description: A real MedlinePlus / NIH (no-auth) LiteratureRetriever behind the literature-retrieval egress axis, confined to the single sanctioned network file (the ESLint exception). retrieve returns disabled|abstain{reason}|grounded{passages}; synthesize runs the single LLM call (local Ollama default) and abstains unless a retrieved passage supports the claim; non-abstained answers carry citations. Source failure => abstain, never fail-open. cloud-inference remains independently off.
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: With literature-retrieval ON, LiteratureRetriever.retrieve queries MedlinePlus and returns grounded passages (tested against a recorded/fixture response — no live network in CI).
AC2: synthesize abstains (abstained=true) when no retrieved passage supports the claim, and every non-abstained clinical claim carries >=1 citation.
AC3: When the literature source is unreachable or errors, retrieve returns abstain{reason} and the engine never fails open (no uncited claim is emitted).

AC4: The only network import in src/medical lives in the designated retrieval file (ESLint boundary passes), and the cloud-inference axis remains independently off (enabling literature-retrieval does not enable cloud inference).

nonFunctionalRequirements.requirement: Local-first zero-egress is the code-enforced default: no outbound network is reachable from src/medical unless the matching EgressGuard axis is opted in, enforced by BOTH a scoped no-restricted-imports ESLint rule and a runtime assertAllowed at every call site (defense in depth).
nonFunctionalRequirements.verificationMethod: ESLint medical-scope boundary passes with network imports only in the sanctioned retrieval file; unit test asserts assertAllowed throws for an off axis; a zero-egress SOP turn reaches no network module.

nonFunctionalRequirements.requirement: The audit log never contains PHI: entries hold IDs and enums only (event, versions, ruleId) — never prompt text or health values — and the file is created mode 0600 with O_APPEND|O_CREAT.
nonFunctionalRequirements.verificationMethod: Unit test serializes audit entries and asserts no prompt/health substrings leak; test asserts file mode 0600 and append-only behavior.

nonFunctionalRequirements.requirement: The emergency red-flag short-circuit and consent refusal are deterministic and make 0 LLM calls; numerical answers come 100% from deterministic in-process compute; clinical claims are cited or abstained — no fail-open path emits an uncited claim.
nonFunctionalRequirements.verificationMethod: Spy LLMClient asserted never invoked on short-circuit/refuse; numerics tests assert no eval/codegen; synthesize tests assert abstain-unless-supported and citation presence.

nonFunctionalRequirements.requirement: Apple Health XML up to ~4GB ingests via streaming SAX with bounded batches and backpressure, never loading the whole document into memory.
nonFunctionalRequirements.verificationMethod: Ingestion test asserts batch-size cap and streaming parse on a fixture; no whole-file read in the adapter.

nonFunctionalRequirements.requirement: All new code is ESM/NodeNext with .js import extensions, strict TS (zero type errors), provider-agnostic (no @anthropic-ai/sdk or openai types outside src/providers/), and adds the medical-sop enum member to PipelineEngineName and its mirrored Zod enums in lockstep.
nonFunctionalRequirements.verificationMethod: Run typecheck + build + lint; grep src/medical for SDK imports (none); test that the enum union and Zod enums agree.

nonFunctionalRequirements.requirement: Byte-zero impact on existing engines and the programming team: ts|skill|workflow and the programming pipeline behave identically after the additive changes.
nonFunctionalRequirements.verificationMethod: Full existing suite stays green; regression test asserts the programming team resolves to its prior engine and selectPipelineEngine(config) is unchanged for ts/skill/workflow.

integrationPoints: PipelineEngineName + PipelineEngine interface: src/orchestrator/workflow/engine.ts:6,9 (add medical-sop member; MedicalSopEngine implements PipelineEngine).

selectPipelineEngine (config path) + selectPipelineEngineForTeam (team path) exhaustive switches: src/orchestrator/workflow/selector.ts:51,103. resolveEngineNameForTeam:75 passes medical-sop through verbatim (no coercion — only 'workflow' is downgraded).
Team.pipelineShape + Team.guardrails slot: src/teams/types.ts:27,29 (guardrails is currently `?: unknown`).
loadTeam(config, teamId?): src/teams/registry.ts:34 (register the built-in 'medical' team alongside 'programming').
pipelineShape Zod enum: src/config/schema.ts:366 + the PipelineSectionSchema engine enum the engine.ts comment mirrors (widen both to include medical-sop).

Detached-spawn contract (ADR-5): src/chat/chat-session.ts (handleTurn, ~247-253), src/chat/run-spawner.ts:111 (fire-and-forget, do NOT await), src/chat/turn-classifier.ts (classify -> spawn), CompletionTailer poll (chat-session.ts ~145,282-287).
FactStore bi-temporal medications (ADR-7): src/state/facts.ts (factId :58-69, schema :136-153, getActiveFacts), src/orchestrator/memory/reconcile.ts:73 (invalidate-don't-delete).
Local model path for synthesize: src/providers/factory.ts (openai-compat/Ollama) via the provider-agnostic LLMClient (src/providers/types.ts:216).
ESLint scoped no-restricted-imports precedent: eslint.config.js telemetry block (~42-69) + src/telemetry/emit.ts:13.
CLI registration mirrors src/cli/commands/run.ts for the new `bober medical import` command.
techNotes.dataModel: New src/medical/ module. HealthObservation { id (SHA-256 of metric|tStart|source|value), metric, value:number, unit, tStart (ISO, a PARAMETER), tEnd?, source }. LabResult { id, biomarker, value, unit, collectedAtIso, referenceLow?, referenceHigh? }. ConsentRecord { consentVersion, acceptedAtIso, rulesetVersion, disclaimerVersion }. AuditEntry { tIso, event: consent|short-circuit|refuse|answer|abstain|ingest, rulesetVersion?, patternsetVersion?, ruleId? } (IDs/enums only). GuardrailVerdict = allow | short-circuit{rule,cannedResponse} | refuse{rule,reason}. MedicalAnswer { body, abstained, citations[], disclaimerFooter, shortCircuit }. NumericResult { primitive, value:number|null, unit, sampleCount }. HealthDataStore = single generic events table (better-sqlite3 sync, INSERT OR IGNORE) mirroring the FactStore single-table convention; medications live in FactStore (bi-temporal) under a medical scope, NOT in HealthDataStore.
securityConsiderations: Refusals/short-circuit/zero-egress/numerics are CODE-ENFORCED, never prompt-only — the highest-stakes decisions never pass through an LLM (FFDCA §201(h) posture).
EgressGuard.assertAllowed gates every network call site at runtime in addition to the static ESLint import boundary (defense in depth against transitive network imports).
Consent is fail-closed: absent consent => refuse before any retrieval or LLM call.
Audit entries hold IDs/enums only (never prompt text or health values); audit file mode 0600, O_APPEND|O_CREAT.
Numerics are a closed whitelist with no eval/codegen/sandbox; a missing computation forces abstain and is added later only as a reviewable code change.

Medications are the FactStore value-of-record (invalidate-don't-delete) so a discontinued/corrected drug supersedes without deletion (audit-preserving); HealthDataStore never stores medication-list state to avoid a divergent second source of truth.
techNotes.existingPatterns: Reuse, do not rebuild: the Team/loadTeam abstraction (src/teams/), the PipelineEngine selector pattern (src/orchestrator/workflow/selector.ts + engine.ts), the detached-spawn chat contract (src/chat/run-spawner.ts:111, chat-session.ts, turn-classifier.ts), the bi-temporal FactStore (src/state/facts.ts, reconcile.ts:73) for medications, the better-sqlite3 single-table sync convention from FactStore for HealthDataStore, the scoped no-restricted-imports ESLint pattern from the telemetry module (eslint.config.js ~42-69), the provider-agnostic LLMClient + Ollama path (src/providers/factory.ts, types.ts:216), the Zod config schema + createDefaultConfig factory (src/config/schema.ts), and CLI command registration (src/cli/commands/run.ts). Architecture: .bober/architecture/arch-20260616-medical-team-architecture.md + ADR-1..7. NOTE: MedicalSopEngine IMPLEMENTS the PipelineEngine interface (engine.ts:9) — the ADR text's `extends` is shorthand.

metadata.riskNotes: High-stakes domain with HARD code-enforced constraints. Risk-first ordering puts the integration linchpin (S1: additive enum across two exhaustive switches + Zod enums, byte-zero impact) and the three code-enforced safety gates (S2 consent/audit, S3 red-flag 0-LLM short-circuit, S6 egress/zero-egress) before the networked slice (S7). Top risks: (1) guardrail bypass if any LLM call precedes the gates — mitigated by routing ALL medical turns through spawn + gates-first in run + the bypass lint rule; (2) egress leak via transitive network import — mitigated by runtime assertAllowed at every call site over the static ESLint glob; (3) 4GB SAX ingestion blocking the loop — mitigated by bounded batches + backpressure; (4) enum addition breaking the exhaustive switches — mitigated by the compile-time never check in both switches; (5) audit PHI leak — mitigated by IDs/enums-only entries + 0600. RELEASE GATE (non-engineering): shipping/enabling is blocked on the external S6.5 FFDCA §201(h) counsel review, which is out of scope for code; this plan is engineering-complete-on-a-branch only.
